name: Squat Reach
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder width apart and keep your chest upright. Lower your body into a squat by bending the knees and pushing the hips back. As you stand up extend the hips and reach both arms overhead then return to start and repeat.
overview: A dynamic squat variation that adds an overhead reach to increase mobility and coordination.
benefits: Improves lower body mobility activates legs and warms up hips and shoulders

name: Jumping Squats
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder width apart and lower into a controlled squat position. Explosively jump upward by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees. Land softly with bent knees then immediately transition into the next squat.
overview: A plyometric squat designed to develop explosive leg power.
benefits: Increases leg power boosts cardiovascular intensity and improves athletic performance

name: Squat Kicks
instructions: Begin standing with feet shoulder width apart and lower into a squat. Rise back up and extend one leg forward in a controlled kick while keeping the core engaged. Return the foot down then repeat the squat and alternate legs.
overview: A dynamic lower body movement combining squats and alternating kicks.
benefits: Improves coordination balance and lower body endurance

name: Burpee Jump
instructions: Start standing then drop into a squat and place hands on the floor. Kick the feet back into a plank position then quickly return the feet toward the hands. Jump upward explosively reaching the arms overhead before starting the next repetition.
overview: A high intensity full body conditioning movement combining squat plank and jump.
benefits: Raises heart rate quickly improves endurance and full body strength

name: Push Ups
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder width. Lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows while keeping the body straight. Push through the palms to extend the arms and return to the starting position.
overview: A classic upper body strength exercise using bodyweight resistance.
benefits: Strengthens chest shoulders triceps and core stability

name: T Plank
instructions: Start in a standard plank position with hands under shoulders and body aligned. Rotate the torso to one side while lifting one arm toward the ceiling forming a T shape with the body. Hold briefly then return to plank and alternate sides.
overview: A plank variation that introduces rotation and shoulder stability work.
benefits: Enhances core stability shoulder control and balance

name: Lunges
instructions: Stand upright and take a step forward with one leg. Lower the hips until both knees are bent roughly at right angles keeping the torso upright. Push through the front heel to return to standing and repeat with the other leg.
overview: A foundational lower body exercise focused on unilateral leg strength.
benefits: Builds leg strength improves balance and enhances mobility

name: Mountain Climbers
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and body straight. Drive one knee toward the chest while keeping the other leg extended. Quickly switch legs in a running motion while maintaining a strong plank posture.
overview: A fast paced core and cardio exercise performed from a plank position.
benefits: Improves cardiovascular fitness core strength and agility

name: Jumping Jacks
instructions: Stand upright with feet together and arms by your sides. Jump the feet outward while raising the arms overhead in a wide arc. Jump back to the starting position and continue rhythmically.
overview: A classic aerobic exercise often used during warm ups.
benefits: Increases heart rate improves coordination and warms the entire body

name: Walking Hip Opener
instructions: Stand upright and step forward with one leg while lifting the knee toward the chest. Rotate the lifted leg outward to open the hip while keeping the torso tall and core engaged. Place the foot down and continue stepping forward repeating the same movement with the opposite leg.
overview: A dynamic warm-up exercise where the hip is lifted and externally rotated while walking to improve mobility.
benefits: Increases hip mobility activates glutes and prepares lower body for movement

name: Split Jump
instructions: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and the other behind while keeping the torso upright. Jump explosively upward by pushing through both legs and switch the leg positions in midair. Land softly in the opposite lunge position with knees bent and immediately prepare for the next repetition.
overview: A plyometric lower body exercise where the athlete jumps from a split lunge and switches legs midair.
benefits: Develops explosive leg power improves coordination and increases cardiovascular intensity

name: Press Up Position Toe Tap
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands placed directly under the shoulders and body in a straight line. Lift one foot slightly off the floor and tap it out to the side while keeping the hips stable and core engaged. Return the foot to the starting position and alternate sides while maintaining a strong plank posture.
overview: A plank variation where one foot taps outward to challenge stability and core control.
benefits: Improves core stability enhances shoulder endurance and develops coordination

name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy box or platform with feet about shoulder width apart. Bend the knees and swing the arms back to generate momentum then jump explosively onto the box. Land softly with both feet on the platform keeping the knees slightly bent then step down carefully before repeating.
overview: A plyometric lower body exercise where the athlete jumps from the ground onto an elevated surface.
benefits: Develops explosive leg power improves coordination and enhances lower body strength

name: Cobra Stretch
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with legs extended behind and palms placed under the shoulders. Press through the hands to lift the chest upward while keeping the hips and legs relaxed on the floor. Hold the position briefly while gently extending the spine then lower the torso back down in a controlled manner.
overview: A floor based spinal extension stretch commonly used in yoga and mobility routines.
benefits: Improves spinal mobility stretches abdominal muscles and helps reduce stiffness from prolonged sitting

name: Punches
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder width apart and keep the knees slightly bent while engaging the core. Extend one arm forward in a straight punch while rotating the torso slightly for power. Quickly retract the arm and alternate punches while maintaining a steady rhythm.
overview: A simple cardio exercise inspired by boxing movements performed without equipment.
benefits: Increases cardiovascular activity improves coordination and strengthens shoulders and arms

name: Wide Arm Push Up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder width on the floor. Lower your chest toward the ground by bending the elbows while keeping the body in a straight line from head to heels. Push through the palms to extend the arms and return to the starting position while maintaining core stability.
overview: A push-up variation where the hands are positioned wider to emphasize chest activation.
benefits: Increases chest engagement strengthens shoulders and improves upper body pushing strength

name: Shoulder Stretch
instructions: Stand upright and extend one arm straight across the chest at shoulder height. Use the opposite hand to gently pull the extended arm closer to the body while keeping the shoulder relaxed. Hold the stretch briefly while breathing normally then switch arms and repeat.
overview: A simple upper body stretch that targets the shoulder muscles and improves joint mobility.
benefits: Improves shoulder flexibility reduces stiffness and helps prevent injury during upper body exercises

name: Seated Abs Circles
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet slightly lifted while leaning the torso slightly back. Place the hands on the floor beside the hips for support and engage the abdominal muscles. Move the knees together in a circular motion while keeping the core tight then reverse the direction after several repetitions.
overview: A seated core stability exercise where the legs move in circular patterns to engage abdominal muscles.
benefits: Strengthens the core improves hip control and enhances abdominal endurance

name: Inchworm
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip width apart and bend forward to place the hands on the floor. Slowly walk the hands forward until the body reaches a high plank position while keeping the core engaged. Walk the feet forward toward the hands keeping the legs relatively straight then return to standing and repeat.
overview: A dynamic mobility exercise that transitions from standing to plank by walking the hands forward.
benefits: Improves hamstring flexibility activates the core and warms up the shoulders and posterior chain

name: Reverse Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back with arms resting beside the body and knees bent with feet lifted off the floor. Engage the core and curl the hips upward lifting the knees toward the chest while keeping the upper back on the floor. Slowly lower the hips back down with control and repeat the movement without using momentum.
overview: A core focused abdominal exercise where the hips lift off the floor to target the lower abdominals.
benefits: Strengthens lower abdominal muscles improves core stability and supports spinal control

name: Step Up on Chair
instructions: Stand facing a stable chair with feet hip width apart and place one foot firmly on the seat. Push through the heel of the elevated foot to lift the body upward until the standing leg is fully extended. Step back down with control and repeat the movement alternating legs.
overview: A functional lower body exercise where the body steps onto an elevated surface using leg strength.
benefits: Builds leg strength improves balance and enhances functional movement ability

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart. Arms rest beside your body. Press through your heels and lift your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Squeeze the glutes at the top, then slowly lower the hips back down. Repeat in a controlled motion.
overview: A bodyweight exercise that strengthens the glutes and posterior chain by lifting the hips from a supine position.
benefits: Strengthens glutes and hamstrings, improves hip stability, supports lower back health, activates posterior chain.

name: Plank Shoulder Tap
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and body in a straight line. Keep feet slightly wider for balance. Lift one hand and tap the opposite shoulder while keeping hips stable. Return the hand to the floor and repeat with the other hand. Continue alternating in a controlled manner.
overview: A core stability exercise performed in a plank position while alternating shoulder taps to challenge balance and anti-rotation control.
benefits: Improves core stability, strengthens shoulders, enhances balance and coordination, trains anti-rotation control.
